column3: Covering arrays for efficient fault characterization in complex configuration spaces
column10: The study is explicitly about post-deployment, runtime fault characterization: it runs tests on deployed systems (ACE+TAO) on Linux and Windows and on remote user sites, analyzes runtime configuration options, and delivers models to developers. The actors and artifacts are operational (remote clients, fielded systems, runtime options/configurations), not design-time variability models.
column11: The paper proposes using covering arrays to efficiently sample large, deployed configuration spaces, executing tests on selected runtime configurations across real platforms, and using classification-tree models to identify and characterize the option settings that induce failures to help developers localize and diagnose faults.

The contribution is about scalable exhaustive pre-deployment analysis of network configs and protocol convergence not post-deployment troubleshooting of misconfigurations in running systems." The paper presents Plankton, a scalable network configuration verifier that combines packet equivalence class partitioning with explicit-state model checking (SPIN) and protocol-specific optimizations to exhaustively explore control-plane convergence and check data-plane policies (e.g., reachability, waypointing, loop freedom) for OSPF, BGP and static routing. Its goal is to detect policy violations in proposed configurations before they are deployed and to outperform prior SMT- and graph-based verifiers on large networks.

column10: The paper focuses on compile-time/static program specialization: it operates on LLVM IR using a user-provided manifest of command-line arguments and configuration file contents to produce a specialized binary. The work targets pre-deployment code debloating (analysis/transforms such as entry-point specialization, file I/O specialization, and constant propagation), not runtime diagnosis, localization, or repair by operators in deployed systems.
column11: It presents TRIMMER, a static-analysis-based system that lifts configuration constants (from command-line inputs and configuration files) into code and applies sparse context-sensitive constant propagation and other transforms to eliminate unused code, reducing binary size, improving performance, and shrinking attack surface.
column21: Aatira Anum Ahmad; Abdul Rafae Noor; Hashim Sharif; Usama Hameed; Shoaib Asif; Mubashir Anwar; Ashish Gehani; Fareed Zaffar; Junaid Haroon Siddiqui

column3: Detecting and Measuring Misconfigured Manifests in Android Apps
column10: The paper studies misconfigurations in deployed Android apps by analyzing AndroidManifest.xml files extracted from real APKs collected from Google Play and pre-installed Samsung firmware, i.e., artifacts in installed/running apps rather than design-time models. It presents an operational detection tool (ManiScope) for operators/researchers to find and explain manifest misconfigurations in the wild.
column11: The paper builds XSD schemas from Android documentation using domain-aware NLP and validates AndroidManifest.xml files in APKs to detect misplaced, missing, unexpected, and misspelled manifest elements and attributes, and measures the prevalence and security impact of these misconfigurations across millions of real-world apps.
column12: ManiScope automatically detects configuration violations using specification constraints, whereas our work focuses on how users diagnose and understand configuration failures in practice.
column16: Proceedings of the 2022 ACM SIGSAC Conference on Computer and Communications Security
column21: Yuqing Yang; Mohamed Elsabagh; Chaoshun Zuo; Ryan Johnson; Angelos Stavrou; Zhiqiang Lin

column3: C2C: Fine-grained Configuration-driven System Call Filtering
column10: The paper focuses on post-deployment, runtime behavior by mapping active runtime configuration options in deployed binaries (Apache, Nginx, Postgres, Wget, etc.) to code and applying Seccomp BPF filters at process launch; the actors are operators/administrators of running systems and the artifacts are real application config objects and system-call profiles.
column11: It presents C2C, a static-analysis-plus-runtime-instrumentation system that maps runtime configuration options to basic-block-level code and generates configuration-specific Seccomp system-call filters to disable system calls used only by disabled features, thereby reducing the user- and kernel-space attack surface of running applications.
column12: The paper focuses on security hardening (attack surface reduction) by filtering system calls
column16: ACM Conference on Computer and Communications Security

column10: The paper targets developers and code reviewers configuring Static Code Analysis Tools (CheckStyle) during development and Continuous Integration; it operates on code review comments and tool configurations (pre-deployment artifacts) rather than on incidents in running/production systems or operator-led runtime diagnosis.
column11: It presents Auto-SCAT, an automated approach that leverages inline code review comments and CheckStyle descriptions to recommend which CheckStyle checks or categories to enable for a project, using textual similarity and a knowledge base, and evaluates accuracy on six Java open-source projects.

column3: AgileCtrl: a self-adaptive framework for configuration tuning
column10: The paper studies post-deployment, runtime configuration tuning and diagnosis in deployed systems (HDFS, HBase, Cassandra), focusing on how a self-adaptive framework (the actor) adjusts live configuration parameters (PerfConfs) and its own internal parameters (AdapConfs) to handle operational mismatches. The evaluations target runtime failures (performance oscillation, OOM, crashes) and runtime resource/workload mismatches, i.e., operational troubleshooting in running systems.
column11: It proposes AgileCtrl, a runtime self-adaptive framework that monitors predicted vs. observed performance and dynamically adjusts both PerfConfs and internal AdapConfs (e.g., model coefficient alpha and virtual goal) to tolerate large model/training errors, prevent oscillations and crashes, and improve performance in deployed systems.
column21: Shu Wang; Henry Hoffmann; Shan Lu

column3: Industry practice of configuration auto-tuning for cloud applications and services
column10: The paper studies auto-tuning of configuration knobs in deployed cloud systems (ECS and ACK) and explicitly targets operators/enterprise users applying changes to live artifacts such as /etc/my.cnf and /proc/sys in running production environments. It focuses on post-deployment operational concerns like safety, distributed deployment, and repeated tuning in live systems.
column11: It identifies practical challenges of applying auto-tuning in production (unsafe knob ranges, insufficient benchmark pressure, lack of distributed tuning support, high time cost, and repetitive tuning) and implements extensions to the KeenTune tool. The proposed solutions include knowledge-based and sensitivity-driven knob selection, automatic benchmark pressure control, distributed/continuous tuning support, and mechanisms to reuse historical tuning profiles to optimize live cloud applications and services.

column3: Misconfiguration-Free Compositional SDN for Cloud Networks
column10: The paper explicitly targets post-deployment SDN policies at the data plane in IaaS cloud networks, analyzing tenant/operator-deployed switch-level match-action rules and compositional operators to find runtime function conflicts and redundant rules in running systems.
column11: It presents PMM, a data-plane misconfiguration checking tool and theory (minimal interval set, composition algebra, action-equivalence) to detect intra- and inter-policy misconfigurationsãfunction conflicts and deployment inefficienciesãin deployed SDN rule sets and compositions, and evaluates its integration with the CoVisor hypervisor.

column3: We're Not Gonna Break It! Consistency-Preserving Operators for Efficient Product Line Configuration
column10: The paper studies automated optimal configuration of software product lines using feature models and search-based techniques, generating consistency-preserving operators and VB-rule encodings for use during genetic search; this is a design-time/product-derivation optimization of feature-model configurations (feature artifacts and offline operator generation), not post-deployment troubleshooting by operators on running systems.
column11: The paper proposes consistency-preserving configuration operators (CPCOs), an efficient algorithm to generate them as variability-based transformation rules, and a tool (aCaPulCO) that uses CPCO-based mutation and crossover to improve the speed and quality of search-based optimal feature-model configuration by avoiding invalid intermediate solutions.

column10: The study analyzes deployed/running Android applications (14.4M apps via AndroidManifest.xml), applies static analysis and a custom attack app on a real device to confirm data leakage, so it addresses misconfigurations in live systems and their exploitation rather than design-time configuration.
column11: It detects and measures insecure content-provider configuration parameters across millions of Android apps, empirically demonstrates practical data leakage via a custom exploit on real devices, and discusses mitigations to help protect user privacy post-deployment.
column12: This work detects misconfigurations through static analysis of application artifacts and demonstrates their exploitability. It does not study diagnosing or repairing configuration failures in deployed systems, and therefore lies outside the scope of misconfiguration troubleshooting.

column3: Evaluating state-of-the-art # SAT solvers on industrial configuration spaces
column10: The paper studies feature models and propositional model counting for product-line configuration (translating feature models to CNF/#SAT) and evaluates solvers for developers/analysts, not for operators troubleshooting running systems; it focuses on design-time variability analyses of feature models rather than post-deployment runtime misconfiguration diagnosis.
column11: Empirically evaluates 21 off-the-shelf #SAT solvers on 130 industrial feature models to assess scalability, identify best-performing solvers and techniques, and analyze correlations between structural metrics and solver runtimes, including evaluation of two approximate model counters.

column3: Can the configuration of static analyses make resolving security vulnerabilities more effective? - A user study
column10: The study explicitly targets SAST tool configuration during development time within IDEs, with developers and security experts as actors and source code/projects as the artifacts; it is about pre-commit, in-IDE use rather than diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in deployed/running systems.
column11: Evaluates whether configuring static-analysis options (rule selection and analysis scope) in the IDE improves users' effectiveness at resolving security findings via a between-subjects user study of 40 participants; reports common user strategies, measures understandability/usefulness of commercial tool options, and provides recommendations for SAST tool creators.

column3: Can Machine Learning Pipelines Be Better Configured?
column10: The paper empirically studies runtime failures of real, deployable ML pipelines (it deploys 3,380 pipelines and runs 74,373 library-version variants) and analyzes performance inconsistencies, crashes and NaN bugs caused by ML library version combinations, reporting results to pipeline authors and library maintainers ã?i.e., it focuses on post-deployment/operator-facing troubleshooting of configuration (library-version) issues on real systems.
column11: It builds Piecer to generate and run library-version variants of ML pipelines to detect and quantify pipeline configuration (PLC) issues (execution time, memory, prediction metrics, crashes/NaNs), characterizes their pervasiveness and root causes, and produces a defective-API repository to help detect and diagnose such runtime misconfigurations.
column12: This work studies how different configuration combinations affect performance and compatibility of ML pipelines, and aims to find better configurations. It does not diagnose or repair configuration failures encountered during system operation, and therefore falls outside the scope of misconfiguration troubleshooting.
column21: Yibo Wang; Ying Wang; Tingwei Zhang; Yue Yu; Shing-Chi Cheung; Hai Yu; Zhiliang Zhu

column3: Pre-training Code Representation with Semantic Flow Graph for Effective Bug Localization
column21: Yali Du; Zhongxing Yu

column3: ConfFix: Repairing Configuration Compatibility Issues in Android Apps
column10: The paper explicitly studies post-deployment/runtime configuration compatibility issues in running Android apps: it diagnoses inconsistent handling of resource XML attributes across Android API levels by replaying UI test scripts and operates on resource XML files used by app developers to fix runtime crashes and UI misrendering. The actors are app developers/operators and the artifact is deployed app XML configuration, with many patches submitted and merged upstream.
column11: It presents ConfFix, an automated approach that diagnoses which Android framework fields are affected by issue-inducing XML attributes and searches for edits to resource XML (adding, changing, or removing attributes/values) to eliminate inconsistent runtime behavior across API levels. The technique was evaluated on 77 reproducible real CC issues and produced many correct, developer-accepted patches.
column12: This work repairs configuration-related defects in application source code during software maintenance. The configuration is part of the program artifact rather than an operational system setting, and the target users are developers rather than operators. Therefore, it falls outside the scope of misconfiguration troubleshooting.
column16: International Symposium on Software Testing and Analysis
column21: Huaxun Huang; Chi Xu; Ming Wen; Yepang Liu; Shing-Chi Cheung

column3: ECSTATIC: Automatic Configuration-Aware Testing and Debugging of Static Analysis Tools
column10: The paper focuses on developers/designers of static analysis tools and describes automated testing and debugging of configurable analyzers run inside Docker; it studies tool-internal configuration testing rather than operators diagnosing or fixing misconfigurations in deployed/running systems or real system configuration files.
column11: It presents ECSTATIC, a framework that uses partial-order relations over static-analysis configuration options to automatically generate configuration pairs, detect violations across analyzer runs, and apply a two-staged, violation-aware delta debugging to reduce failure-inducing input programs.
column16: International Symposium on Software Testing and Analysis
column21: Austin Mordahl; Dakota Soles; Miao Miao; Zenong Zhang; Shiyi Wei

column3: Colla-Config: A stakeholders preferences-based approach for product lines collaborative configuration
column10: The paper focuses on design-time collaborative software product-line configuration where stakeholders select features from feature models and resolve conflicts during product derivation; actors are product managers/engineers working on feature models, not operators of running systems. The artifacts are feature models and substitution rules for conflict resolution at configuration time, so it does not address post-deployment operational troubleshooting of live system config files or runtime incidents.
column11: It proposes Colla-Config, a free-order collaborative configuration approach that elicits stakeholder preferences as substitution rules and resolves conflicts by computing Minimal Correction Subsets (MCSs) to remove minimal conflicting feature selections; the work includes a tool implementation and a usability study.
column21: Sihem Ben Sassi; Sabrine Edded; Raû¤l Mazo; Henda Ben Ghûˋzala; Camille Salinesi

column3: Practical Intent-driven Routing Configuration Synthesis
column10: The paper studies post-deployment, operational configuration management in running production datacenters: it is deployed for over two years in Meta datacenters and targets network operators managing BGP and switch configurations, switch states, and live production traffic and reconÿ˜gurations.
column11: It presents Aura, an intent-driven routing configuration synthesis system (RPL language, compiler, staging and labeling) that generates and stages BGP/switch configurations for live networks to support dynamic and conditional routing policies, minimize disruptive reconfiguration, and reduce operator burden.
column12: Prior work synthesizes configurations from operator intent to prevent errors, whereas our work studies how operators diagnose configuration failures after deployment.
column16: Symposium on Network System Design and Implementation

column3: Test Coverage for Network Configurations
column10: The paper studies post-deployment, operational network configurations in running systems (case study on the Internet2 backbone and synthetic datacenter networks), targets network engineers/operators as the actor, and reasons about real configuration files, protocol RIBs/main RIB and data-plane state to identify tested vs untested configuration lines.
column11: It presents NetCov, a tool that computes which network configuration lines are covered by a suite of data- and control-plane tests by lazily building an information-flow graph that maps configuration elements to runtime data-plane facts, accounts for non-local and non-deterministic contributions, and produces coverage reports to guide operators in improving test suites.
column12: NetCov improves configuration testing quality, whereas our work studies how operators diagnose configuration failures after deployment.
column16: Symposium on Network System Design and Implementation
column21: Xieyang Xu; Weixin Deng; Ryan Beckett; Ratul Mahajan; David Walker

column3: Automated Firewall Configuration in Virtual Networks
column10: The paper addresses automatic synthesis and orchestration of firewall allocation and rule generation for a given virtual Service Graph and produces configurations to be deployed, i.e., a pre-deployment/engineering solution. The work is framed around a service designer/operator generating an allocation graph and generating configs prior to embedding and deployment rather than diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in running production systems by operators.
column11: It formulates automated allocation and auto-configuration of distributed packet-filter firewalls in a virtual Service Graph as a partial weighted MaxSMT problem and outputs optimal allocation points and abstract filtering policies, minimizing number of firewalls and rules while providing formal correctness guarantees.
column16: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
column21: Daniele Bringhenti; Guido Marchetto; Riccardo Sisto; Fulvio Valenza; Jalolliddin Yusupov

column3: Do Performance Aspirations Matter for Guiding Software Configuration Tuning? An Empirical Investigation under Dual Performance Objectives
column10: The paper explicitly studies post-deployment configuration tuning by measuring and profiling running systems and frames the actors as stakeholders/operators negotiating requirements and software engineers/operators tuning configurations; experiments are performed on real-world deployed systems (Storm, x264, Keras, XGBoost) with measurements on the actual configurable system. It evaluates operator-facing troubleshooting actions (how to guide tuning and select configurations) rather than design-time validation or compile-time checks.
column11: It empirically investigates whether and when to incorporate stakeholder-specified performance aspirations into bi-objective Pareto-based configuration tuning for running systems, comparing 'Pareto search with aspirations' versus 'Pareto search without aspirations' across many requirement patterns, aspiration positions, optimizers, systems, and tuning budgets.
column12: This work optimizes configuration settings to satisfy performance requirements, whereas our study focuses on diagnosing and understanding configuration failures encountered by users in practice.

column3: Fuzzing Configurations of Program Options
column10: The paper studies configuration-aware fuzzing in testing campaigns (pre-deployment), driven by test engineers and fuzzers operating on program command-line options and grammars; it focuses on transforming programs for improved test coverage rather than on post-deployment/operator troubleshooting of live system configs.
column11: It proposes ConfigFuzz, a technique and tool that encodes program options into an expanded fuzzing input so existing fuzzers can mutate configurations and normal inputs together to find more code paths and bugs, and evaluates this approach against baseline fuzzing strategies.
column16: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
column21: Zenong Zhang; George Klees; Eric Wang; Michael Hicks; Shiyi Wei

column3: Fuzzing Configurations of Program Options - RCR Report
column10: The paper studies fuzz testing of program option configurations in an experimental/testing context (using FuzzBench and AFL on targets like FFmpeg and xmllint) and focuses on researcher-run fuzz campaigns on transformed programs and configuration grammars, not on operators diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in running/production systems.
column11: It presents ConfigFuzz, a technique that encodes program options into configuration bytes so a fuzzer can mutate both configurations and input data, evaluates its impact on code coverage across several configurable programs, and provides artifacts and scripts to reproduce the experiments.
column16: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
column21: Zenong Zhang; George Klees; Eric Wang; Michael Hicks; Shiyi Wei

column3: Learning Configurations of Operating Environment of Autonomous Vehicles to Maximize their Collisions
column10: The paper studies automated test-generation by configuring simulated operating-environment parameters (weather, pedestrians, NPC vehicles) via REST APIs in the LGSVL simulator and evaluates against an autopilot (Apollo); the work is about adversarial scenario generation in simulation by researchers, not about operators diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in deployed/running systems or real production config files.
column11: It proposes DeepCollision, a Deep Q-Learning approach that learns dynamic environment configurations to maximize collision probability for testing autonomous driving systems, formalizing the problem as an MDP, implementing REST API endpoints to manipulate simulator parameters, and evaluating models across different roads and time-interval settings.
column21: Chengjie Lu; Yize Shi; Huihui Zhang; Man Zhang; Tiexin Wang; Tao Yue; Shaukat Ali

column3: CfgNet: A Framework for Tracking Equality-Based Configuration Dependencies Across a Software Project
column10: The paper is developer-focused and explicitly targets proactive prevention during development and maintenance (e.g., running as Git hooks) by analyzing repository configuration artifacts like Dockerfiles and pom.xml, not diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in running/production systems by operators.
column11: It presents CFGNET, a plugin-based framework that models configuration artifacts across a project as a network and uses an equality-based linker to detect and track cross-technology configuration dependencies, flagging inconsistent changes and suggesting fixes.
column21: Sebastian Simon; Nicolai Ruckel; Norbert Siegmund

column3: Multi-Misconfiguration Diagnosis via Identifying Correlated Configuration Parameters
column10: The paper explicitly targets post-deployment configuration errors and assists software users/operators when 'a configuration error occurs' by using test-execution based suspiciousness and static program-slicing correlation analysis; it evaluates on reproduced real-world runtime misconfigurations from StackOverflow and live Java projects. The approach operates over real system code and execution traces to localize and recommend multiple misconfigured runtime parameters (i.e., config files/parameters) for repair.
column11: It presents MMD, a tool that identifies correlations among configuration parameters via program-slicing-based vector representations and distance metrics, then combines those correlations with test-execution suspiciousness rankings to diagnose and prioritize multiple root-cause configuration parameters in running systems.
column21: Yingnan Zhou; Xue Hu; Sihan Xu; Yan Jia; Yuhao Liu; Junyong Wang; Guangquan Xu; Wei Wang; Shaoying Liu; Thar Baker

column3: Face It Yourselves: An LLM-Based Two-Stage Strategy to Localize Configuration Errors via Logs
column10: The paper explicitly studies post-deployment, runtime troubleshooting: it analyzes and uses logs from deployed Hadoop systems and targets end-users/operators (including those without source-code access) to localize misconfigured configuration properties in running systems.
column11: It proposes an LLM-based two-stage method and tool (LogConfigLocalizer) that first identifies anomalous log messages and then directly or indirectly infers the root-cause configuration properties from provided configuration settings and logs, evaluated on Hadoop with high reported accuracy.
column12: This work localizes configuration errors from runtime logs to assist end-users in diagnosing and fixing misconfigurations. It directly targets post-deployment troubleshooting and therefore falls within our study scope.
column16: International Symposium on Software Testing and Analysis
column21: Shiwen Shan; Yintong Huo; Yuxin Su; Yichen Li; Dan Li; Zibin Zheng

column3: Understanding Misconfigurations in ROS: An Empirical Study and Current Approaches
column10: The paper focuses on misconfigurations that are discovered during deployment and at run-time (it repeatedly cites errors \discovered during field deployment\" and analyzes runtime artifacts such as Launch XML and ROS Parameter YAML). It studies operator/developer troubleshooting evidence from ROS Answers Q&A posts about running systems and evaluates tools that detect/diagnose misconfiguration in deployed ROS systems."
column11: The paper empirically derives a taxonomy (12 high-level categories, 50 subcategories) of misconfigurations in ROS from ROS Answers and provides a dataset of labeled questions. It then conducts a literature review mapping existing static and dynamic analysis techniques to these misconfiguration types, identifying gaps (27 subcategories not covered) and outlining directions for tooling to detect/diagnose runtime misconfigurations.
column12: This work empirically categorizes misconfiguration types and surveys existing techniques. It does not diagnose or repair configuration failures, and therefore falls outside the scope of misconfiguration troubleshooting.
column16: International Symposium on Software Testing and Analysis

column3: DValidator: An approach for validating dependencies in build configurations
column10: The work targets build-time validation and generation of Maven build configurations (project POMs) using static dependency and call graphs and explicitly addresses developers/build engineers and IDE integration, not runtime operators or troubleshooting of deployed systems. The artifacts and techniques are about pre-deployment dependency management (build configurations, ASP models, Clingo), so it does not study post-deployment misconfiguration diagnosis in running systems.
column11: It encodes a project's dependency graph and method-level call graph into Answer Set Programming and uses the Clingo solver to detect dependency 'smells' (e.g., duplicated, shadowed, jump calls, unused dependencies) and to validate or generate well-formed build dependency configurations.
column21: Christian Macho; Fabian Oraéƒe; Martin Pinzger

column3: ETune: Efficient configuration tuning for big-data software systems via configuration space reduction
column10: The paper addresses runtime configuration tuning for deployed big-data systems (Spark) evaluated on a cluster: it uses performance observations from past tuning tasks and iterative evaluations of live workloads, with operators/automatic tuners acting in an operational (post-deployment) context and the artifact being running-system configuration parameters.
column11: It proposes ETune, a Bayesian-Optimization-based automatic tuner that reduces the configuration space via parameter-selection-in-the-loop and transfer-learning of promising regions across past workloads to more efficiently find high-performance configurations under workload changes.
column12: ETune searches for high-performance configurations using tuning history and workload similarity, whereas our work focuses on diagnosing configuration failures encountered by users in running systems.
column21: Cao Rong; Liang Bao; Kaibi Zhao; Panpan Zhangsun

column3: MadEye: Boosting Live Video Analytics Accuracy with Adaptive Camera Configurations
column10: The paper describes a runtime camera-server system that automatically adapts PTZ camera orientations in deployed live video analytics (operator-facing, post-deployment) but its primary focus is on online optimization and control of camera orientation/zoom for accuracy and resource tradeoffs, not on diagnosing, localizing, or repairing configuration errors or operator troubleshooting of misconfigurations.
column11: It presents MadEye, a system that continually explores and ranks PTZ camera orientations using on-camera approximation models and an on-camera search algorithm to maximize analytics (DNN) accuracy under network and compute resource constraints.
column16: Symposium on Network System Design and Implementation
column21: Mike Wong; Murali Ramanujam; Guha Balakrishnan; Ravi Netravali

column3: OPPerTune: Post-Deployment Configuration Tuning of Services Made Easy
column10: The paper explicitly studies post-deployment, operator-facing configuration tuning for running systems: it targets operators, runs on deployed/production applications on Microsoft Azure, and uses live workload rewards from running services to drive tuning.
column11: It designs and deploys OPPerTune, a service that automates post-deployment configuration tuning (including selecting which parameters to tune, scoping instances via AutoScope, and tuning hybrid numeric/categorical spaces via HybridBandits) to improve runtime performance (e.g., P95 latency) while reducing disruptions and sample complexity.
column12: OPPerTune optimizes configuration values to improve performance after deployment, whereas our work studies how practitioners diagnose configuration failures after deployment.
column16: Symposium on Network System Design and Implementation

column3: MobileConfig: Remote Configuration Management for Mobile Apps at Hyperscale
column10: This paper is an operational study of a production remote-configuration system used since 2015 to manage configs for apps running on billions of devices; it explicitly addresses post-deployment/operator concerns such as detecting and diagnosing config divergence, preventing misconfigurations with multi-stage canaries, and rapidly fixing faults via emergency push on live mobile clients.
column11: Designs and evaluates MobileConfig, a hyperscale remote-configuration management system that balances fast app startup and fresh runtime config via progressive consistency and provides operational tooling (canaries, consistency checking, emergency push) to detect, prevent, and repair misconfigurations in deployed mobile apps.
column12: MobileConfig prevents and mitigates configuration errors via deployment safeguards, whereas our work studies how practitioners diagnose configuration failures after deployment.

column10: The paper explicitly targets post-deployment, real-time diagnosis of configuration errors in running AI server infrastructure (evaluated on Hadoop), mapping configuration read sites to log outputs and parsing live logs to identify problematic options; the intended actor is the operator/user diagnosing runtime misconfigurations. It operates on production artifacts (real-time logs and deployed configuration options) rather than design-time models.
column11: The paper presents a method that automatically locates configuration read sites, performs static program slicing once to map configuration options to log-producing code, and then parses and clusters real-time logs to match WARN/ERROR messages to likely misconfigured options, enabling timely diagnosis of runtime configuration errors in deployed systems.

column3: The Potential Harm of Email Delivery: Investigating the HTTPS Configurations of Webmail Services
column10: The paper performs active measurements of HTTPS-related configurations on live webmail servers and client browsers (21k webmail domains, 27M domains derived from 2.2B addresses, and 50 browser/OS combinations), explicitly studying deployed/running systems and operator/browser-vendor behavior rather than design-time modeling. It analyzes real operational artifacts (server TLS settings, HTTP headers, certificates, TLSA/DNSSEC, OCSP/CRL/Stapling, CT, SCSV) that affect post-deployment security.
column11: The paper measures and quantifies deployment and misconfiguration of HTTPS security features in webmail servers and browsers at Internet scale, classifies server security levels, and identifies practical vulnerabilities (e.g., invalid certificates, missing HSTS/OCSP stapling, DANE non-deployment, downgrade risks) that threaten email delivery confidentiality and integrity.
column12: Prior studies measured widespread configuration mistakes in real-world systems, but they did not investigate how practitioners diagnose and understand such failures.

It targets live telecom infrastructure and operator/manufacturer behavior rather than design-time models or build-time checks.
column11: It identifies and analyzes exploitable configuration and implementation vulnerabilities in deployed LTE/IMS networks that can disable integrity/confidentiality protections, demonstrates practical PoC attacks (IMEI/phone catching, SMS and call impersonation/interception) on real networks, and discusses mitigations and responsible disclosure.
column12: Prior security studies showed that misconfigurations can lead to severe vulnerabilities and attacks, but they do not study how practitioners diagnose such configuration problems in practice.

It addresses sampling, modeling, and search over colossal configuration spaces rather than operator-led diagnosis, localization, or repair of misconfigurations in running/operational systems.
column11: It presents uniform random sampling (BDDSampler), order-statistics-based statistical guarantees (e.g., a returned configuration is within x% of optimal with y% confidence), Recursive Random Search and fixed-budget strategies to find near-optimal configurations in colossal software product-line configuration spaces, and empirical comparisons to performance-model approaches.

Model of Software Configuration to MSCv2 by adding one new dimension (Activation Strategy) and 25 new values, and demonstrates applicability by creating MSCv2 instances for research publications and the Chrome repository to standardize definitions and guide future research on software configuration.

column10: The paper focuses on design-time product-line feature configuration performed by developers using requirement/problem-world/specification feature models and tools, i.e., pre-deployment configuration of software variants rather than operator diagnosis or repair of misconfigurations in running systems.

column11: The paper proposes an approach to improve the efficiency and quality of product configuration in software product lines by optimizing the feature selection process; it defines measures such as positive/negative coverage and configuration coverage to guide configuration decisions.

The work addresses view-to-workflow mapping, concurrent configuration management, persistence/recovery, and tooling to scale interactive product derivation in software product lines.

column11: The paper presents Invar, a web-service-based infrastructure and a set of configuration primitives and IMDI inter-model dependency links to integrate heterogeneous variability models so end-users can configure products across multi product lines; it includes prototype integrations with FaMa and DOPLER and scenario-based validation.

column11: It proposes a reference architecture and a FeatureIDE-based prototype that generates product variants from feature models, analyzes per-variant architectural quality properties (safety, schedulability, resource use), stores per-variant metrics in a variant inventory, and provides tool support to help engineers explore quality trade-offs during product configuration.

column10: The paper focuses on variability management and configurator design from feature models and historical configuration logs (configuration traces), targeting domain engineers building configurators rather than operators troubleshooting running systems. It studies workflow discovery from configurator logs and feature-models, not post-deployment diagnosis or repair of misconfigurations in production artifacts or services.
column11: It proposes COLOSSI, a process-mining plus clustering approach that takes a feature model and raw configuration logs to discover and simplify configuration workflows so domain engineers can build better configurators. The technique is validated on an ERP feature-model dataset to show reduced workflow complexity and improved understandability.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Ángel Jesús Varela-Vaca; José Angel Galindo; Belén Ramos-Gutiérrez; María Teresa Gómez-López; David Benavides

column3: Towards quality assurance of software product lines with adversarial configurations
column10: The paper examines adversarially generated configurations within a Software Product Line (SPL) during product derivation and ML-based QA of an industrial video generator (MOTIV); it focuses on improving variability models and testing oracles in the development/derivation stage rather than on post-deployment troubleshooting of running systems by operators. The actors and artifacts are SPL engineers, variability models, and derived video products, not operators diagnosing live system misconfigurations.
column11: It develops and evaluates a gradient-based evasion attack to synthesize adversarial configurations that fool an ML classifier predicting whether derived videos are acceptable, measures attack effectiveness and validity with respect to the variability model, and discusses leveraging adversarial examples to improve classifier robustness, the variability model, and the testing oracle.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Paul Temple; Mathieu Acher; Gilles Perrouin; Battista Biggio; Jean-Marc Jézéquel; Fabio Roli

column3: Uniform random sampling product configurations of feature models that have numerical features
column10: This work samples valid configurations in a feature model to enable statistical reasoning and optimization. It does not address configuration failures or their diagnosis and repair, and therefore falls outside the scope of misconfiguration troubleshooting.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Daniel-Jesus Munoz; Jeho Oh; Mónica Pinto; Lidia Fuentes; Don S. Batory

column3: HADAS: analysing quality attributes of software configurations
column10: The paper focuses on design-time Software Product Line variability modelling and developer-facing decision support (NVMs, solvers, a cloud NFQA repository, IDE plugins and web views) rather than diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in deployed/running systems. It targets developers selecting optimal product variants using measured NFQAs, not post-deployment operator troubleshooting of live systems.
column11: HADAS links variability models (complete valid configurations) to a repository of non-functional quality-attribute measurements and uses solvers and statistical analysis to recommend optimal configurations (e.g., fastest, greenest, balanced) via web UI, microservice, and IDE plugins to aid developers in configuration selection.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Daniel-Jesus Munoz; Mónica Pinto; Lidia Fuentes

column3: Enabling efficient automated configuration generation and management
column10: The paper focuses on automated configuration generation and management for software product lines using feature models and SAT solvers, targeting developers and end-users during configuration, testing, and product-generation phases rather than operators of running systems; it treats artifacts like feature models, modal implication graphs, and sampling algorithms (design/build-time), not post-deployment runtime troubleshooting or operational configs.
column11: The paper addresses scalability and efficiency of semi- and fully-automatic configuration processes for large and evolving feature models by proposing algorithms and data structures (e.g., modal implication graphs, IncLing) to speed up decision propagation, uniform random sampling, t-wise interaction sampling, and updating configuration samples after feature-model evolution.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Sebastian Krieter

column3: Test them all, is it worth it?: assessing configuration sampling on the JHipster web development stack (Conference)
column10: This work detects failures caused by feature interactions through exhaustive configuration testing during development. It does not diagnose or repair configuration errors in deployed systems and therefore falls outside the scope of misconfiguration troubleshooting.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Axel Halin; Alexandre Nuttinck; Mathieu Acher; Xavier Devroey; Gilles Perrouin; Benoit Baudry

column3: Real-time-aware reconfiguration decisions for dynamic software product lines
column10: The paper focuses on design-time/specification and formal analysis of Dynamic Software Product Line (DSPL) reconfiguration constraints: developers write feature models and RRCL constraints which are translated to timed automata and checked with Uppaal. It is about ensuring real-time reconfiguration consistency in models rather than operator-led, post-deployment diagnosis or repair of misconfigurations on running systems or config files in production.
column11: The paper proposes a feature-oriented language (RRCL) to express real-time constraints for DSPL reconfiguration decisions and a compositional translation into timed automata to automatically analyze consistency (deadlock freedom, reachability, liveness) of reconfiguration behavior; it implements a prototype tool and evaluates it on case studies and synthetic models.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Hendrik Göttmann; Lars Luthmann; Malte Lochau; Andy Schürr

column3: Policy-driven middleware for multi-tenant saas services configuration
column10: The paper focuses on SPLE-based policy generation and a Configurator metamodel to adapt multi-tenant SaaS instances, emphasizing domain engineering, model-to-model transformations, and design-time variability management; while it mentions runtime adaptation via annotations, it does not investigate post-deployment troubleshooting, operator diagnosis, or repair of misconfigurations in running systems. The actors and artifacts studied are SLA models and generated policies/configurators rather than operators diagnosing production incidents on real deployed systems.
column11: It proposes a policy-driven middleware architecture that models generic and tenant-specific SLAs, generates policies, and provides a Configurator component/metamodel to adapt SaaS services to tenant-specific functional and non-functional requirements. The approach supports configuration at design time and runtime through annotations to enable dynamic adaptation of quality attributes across tenants.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Khadija Aouzal; Hatim Hafiddi; Mohamed Dahchour

column3: Requirements-driven configuration of emergency response missions with small aerial vehicles
column10: The paper explicitly focuses on pre-launch, requirements-driven product-line configuration for multi-UAV emergency missions (feature models, activity diagrams, and a configurator that emits mission JSON) used by emergency responders before deployment; it emphasizes design-time / configuration-time artifacts and workflows rather than diagnosing, localizing, or repairing misconfigurations in running/production systems.
column11: It presents a process and toolchain to elicit use cases, construct and merge mission-specific feature models and activity diagrams into a software product line, and a configurator that generates mission-specific configurations and visualizations for semi-autonomous UAV emergency response missions, validated with tests and a user study.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Jane Cleland-Huang; Ankit Agrawal; Md Nafee Al Islam; Eric Tsai; Maxime Van Speybroeck; Michael Vierhauser

column3: GuyDance: Guiding Configuration Updates for Product-Line Evolution
column10: The paper explicitly targets application engineers updating configurations "used in the field" after product-line evolution and frames the actors as domain engineers (who evolve product lines) and application engineers (operators) who must update deployed configuration artifacts; it focuses on post-deployment configuration update and preserving product behavior of generated products.
column11: It presents GuyDance, a formal methodology, guidance templates, and a prototype tool enabling domain engineers to define automated/semi-automated/manual update operations so application engineers can update deployed product-line configurations after evolution, with formal proofs of behavior preservation for example templates.
column12: GuyDance addresses updating configurations after product-line evolution, while our work focuses on diagnosing and explaining configuration mistakes encountered by users in running systems.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Michael Nieke; Gabriela Cunha Sampaio; Thomas Thüm; Christoph Seidl; Leopoldo Teixeira; Ina Schaefer

column3: Evaluating recommender systems in feature model configuration
column10: The paper focuses on feature model development and configurator usage (feature model analysis, configuration, and reconfiguration) and explicitly targets evaluation of recommenders for feature models and configurators, i.e., a design-time/variability-modelling context with developers/users, not operators troubleshooting runtime configurations in deployed systems. It centers on artifacts like feature models and constraint-based configuration tasks rather than post-deployment config files or operational incidents in running systems.
column11: The paper presents evaluation techniques and metrics for recommender systems integrated with feature model configurators, defining measures (e.g., classification/prediction accuracy, consistency, optimality) for scenarios of feature model analysis, configuration, and reconfiguration to compare and standardize recommendation quality.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Mathias Uta; Alexander Felfernig; Viet Man Le; Andrei Popescu; Thi Ngoc Trang Tran; Denis Helic

column3: Optimisation for the product configuration system of Renault: towards an integration of symmetries
column10: The paper focuses on offline knowledge compilation and product-line variability modeling for Renault's configurator (cluster trees, CSP/SAT instances) and on compressing the compiled configuration space using symmetry detection; it targets compilation/memory optimization rather than post-deployment operator troubleshooting of running systems. The actors and artifacts discussed are configurator engineers and the compiled configuration space for online customer selection, not operators diagnosing misconfigurations in deployed services.
column11: It aims to reduce memory pressure of Renault's product configuration system by detecting and exploiting symmetries in variability models to compress the compiled configuration space and by proposing modules (symmetry detection, application, and solution restoration) and parameter tuning strategies to preserve fast online response.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Hao Xu; Souheib Baarir; Tewfik Ziadi; Lom-Messan Hillah; Siham Essodaigui; Yves Bossu

column3: Accuracy- and consistency-aware recommendation of configurations.
column10: The paper investigates integrating recommender systems into constraint-based configurators and feature models during interactive product configuration sessions (users selecting features) and operates on configuration knowledge bases and solver heuristics rather than on deployed/running systems or operator incident diagnosis. It targets configurator users and product-line artifacts, not post-deployment operators or runtime service misconfiguration.
column11: It presents methods to encode ML-based recommendations (kNN and feed-forward neural networks) into constraint solver search heuristics and to initialize NN weights from domain constraints to produce accurate, consistency-preserving configuration recommendations, evaluated on an industrial high-voltage switchgear configuration dataset.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Mathias Uta; Alexander Felfernig; Denis Helic; Viet-Man Le

column3: Feature subset selection for learning huge configuration spaces: the case of linux kernel size
column10: The paper focuses on building and measuring Linux kernel binaries from .config files and using machine learning to predict binary size at compile-time (pre-deployment), aimed at developers/users who tune configurations rather than operators diagnosing running systems. It studies feature selection and prediction on a dataset of measured builds, not post-deployment misconfiguration detection or runtime troubleshooting in production systems.
column11: The paper automates prediction of Linux kernel vmlinux binary size from thousands of Kconfig options by building and measuring 95,854 configurations (TuxML) and applying tree-based feature selection. It shows that a reduced subset of options yields faster, more interpretable and more accurate ML models for predicting kernel size.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Mathieu Acher; Hugo Martin; Luc Lesoil; Arnaud Blouin; Jean-Marc Jézéquel; Djamel Eddine Khelladi; Olivier Barais; Juliana Alves Pereira

column3: We're not gonna break it!: consistency-preserving operators for efficient product line configuration
column10: The paper studies automated optimal configuration of software product lines using feature models and search-based techniques, generating consistency-preserving operators and VB-rule encodings for use during genetic search; this is a design-time/product-derivation optimization of feature-model configurations (feature artifacts and offline operator generation), not post-deployment troubleshooting by operators on running systems.
column11: The paper proposes consistency-preserving configuration operators (CPCOs), an efficient algorithm to generate them as variability-based transformation rules, and a tool (aCaPulCO) that uses CPCO-based mutation and crossover to improve the speed and quality of search-based optimal feature-model configuration by avoiding invalid intermediate solutions.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: José Miguel Horcas; Daniel Strüber; Alexandru Burdusel; Jabier Martinez; Steffen Zschaler

column3: 24th International Workshop on Configuration (CONFWS'22).
column10: The text describes a workshop focused on design-time configuration research (knowledge-representation formalisms, feature models, configurators, and reasoning methods) aimed at developing and maintaining configuration models rather than diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in deployed/running systems by operators. It targets researchers, configurator vendors, and product-line engineers, not post-deployment operators troubleshooting live services.
column11: The workshop promotes research and exchange on configuration problems, models, reasoning methods, and toolsãsuch as feature models, constraint solving, and configuratorsãto support the development, maintenance, and automation of configuration knowledge bases and product-line configurators.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Cipriano Forza; José A. Galindo; Martin Stettinger; Élise Vareilles

column3: BEEHIVE: behaviour-induced configuration of high variability-intensive systems
column10: The paper studies design-time, simulation-based configuration of Cyber-Physical Systems using Simulink models by engineers, exploring parameter ranges and injecting noises/disturbances in models; it analyzes variants before deployment rather than operating on running systems. There is no evidence of operator-focused diagnosis, localization, or repair of misconfigurations in production/deployed systems.
column11: It presents BEEHIVE, an approach to explore the configuration space of high-variability CPS via simulation campaigns with injected noises and disturbances to identify valid configuration ranges and assess robustness. The goal is to find configurations that satisfy mission goals, assurance levels, and budget constraints and to reduce simulation cost via snapshot heuristics.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Valeria Trombetta

column3: Acapulco: an extensible tool for identifying optimal and consistent feature model configurations
column10: The paper studies design-time/product-line feature model configuration and optimization (artifact: feature models and derived variants) within FeatureIDE, targeting domain/application engineers and researchers rather than operators of running systems; it does not address post-deployment/runtime misconfiguration diagnosis or repair in operational environments.
column11: It presents Acapulco, a search-based optimization tool that uses consistency-preserving configuration operators (CPCOs) with IBEA to generate optimal and valid feature-model configurations, offers extensible objective functions, and supports handling complex cross-tree constraints.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Jabier Martinez; Daniel Strüber; José Miguel Horcas; Alexandru Burdusel; Steffen Zschaler

column3: Configuration of domotic systems based on constraint solving
column10: The paper focuses on pre-deployment planning: it presents a constraint-solving configurator used by end-users and local service providers to plan and select smart-home components and explicitly states it is not about run-time/usage. The actor is the consumer/installer and the artifact is the planned configuration of devices (voltages, connectivity, wiring), not troubleshooting of deployed systems.
column11: It presents a prototype smart-home configurator backed by the Choco constraint solver that models technical constraints (e.g., voltage, current type, connectivity) and guides end-users through a dialogue-based process to select compatible, integrable smart-home components and solutions.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Gerhard Leitner; Martin Stettinger

column3: Applying incremental answer set solving to product configuration
column10: The paper studies product configuration in industrial/customizable products using incremental Answer Set Programming and models user actions and deterministic configuration algorithms; it addresses constructing valid configurations of domain artifacts (racks, frames, modules) in an interactive/design-time setting rather than diagnosing or repairing configurations in running/production systems by operators.
column11: It applies incremental/multi-shot ASP (CLINGO) to build product configurations stepãbyãstep, proposing several encodings (generic, ordered, algorithmic, UI) to control nonãdeterminism and improve scalability, and evaluates their performance on synthetic hardware rack instances.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Richard Comploi-Taupe; Giulia Francescutto; Gottfried Schenner

column3: ConGuess: a learning environment for configuration tasks
column10: The paper focuses on an educational Android app for students/learners to practice constraint-solving and configuration semantics using generated CSP puzzles, i.e., a pre-deployment learning context rather than post-deployment operational troubleshooting. The actors are learners not operators and the artifacts are generated CSP instances, not real-world running system configuration files.
column11: It proposes ConGuess, a gamified environment that generates CSP instances (including n-ary and configuration domain-specific constraints), measures their difficulty using a solver's search-tree depth, and lets learners solve and validate solutions in-app to learn configuration rule semantics.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Andreas Hofbauer; Alexander Felfernig

column3: AI-implemented toolkit to assist users with career "configuration" the case of create your own future
column10: The paper investigates a web-based AI career 'service configurator' for individual job seekers and coaches, focusing on user skill assessment and personalized career roadmaps rather than post-deployment software configuration or operational troubleshooting. The actors are learners and human advisors and the artifacts are career profiles, AI mentors, and training recommendations ã?not runtime system config files or production incidents.
column11: It presents the design, implementation, and evaluation of Create Your Own Future (CYOF), an AI-enabled toolkit that assesses users' transferable skills, matches them to job families and job profiles, recommends training, provides AI mentors with voice and sentiment analysis, and helps users build personalized action plans to improve career adaptability.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Chiara Grosso; Noorie Sazen; Roberto Boselli

column3: A meta-model for product configuration ontologies
column10: The paper focuses on design-time conceptual modelling of product configuration via ontologies and a meta-model for configuration models and configurators, referencing artefacts like ontologies and configuration models and actors such as developers, domain experts, and customers; it does not study post-deployment runtime incidents, operators, or troubleshooting of live system configuration files.
column11: It synthesizes existing product configuration ontologies into a meta-model that captures configuration types (components, ports, resources, functions), product compositional structure, and a constraint hierarchy to improve reuse and knowledge sharing for configurator systems, and proposes extensions such as optimal configuration and sustainability information.
column12: multi dimention
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Ebrahim Khalil Abbasi; Tony Leclercq; Patrick Heymans

column3: Consistency-based integration of multi-stakeholder recommender systems with feature model configuration
column10: The paper focuses on design-time feature model configuration and multi-stakeholder preference aggregation (modeling feature selections as CSPs and computing repairs), addressing product-line/configurator scenarios rather than post-deployment runtime incidents. It targets stakeholders using configurators to make joint decisions over feature models, not operators diagnosing misconfigurations in running systems or production configs.
column11: It defines multi-stakeholder feature model configuration as a CSP, detects minimal conflict sets, and computes repair actions via model-based diagnosis. It integrates social-choice preference-aggregation heuristics to recommend which repairs to apply so the group reaches a consistent feature configuration.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Viet-Man Le; Thi Ngoc Trang Tran; Alexander Felfernig

column3: Knowledge-based configuration of videos using feature models
column10: The paper focuses on feature-model based, user-driven configuration of personalized videos (pre-delivery content synthesis) involving end users and CSP encodings of feature selections; it does not study operators diagnosing or repairing runtime configuration faults in deployed systems or configuration files in production.
column11: It defines video variability using feature models and formulates the configuration task as a constraint satisfaction problem to synthesize personalized (playlist or interactive) videos from existing segments based on user knowledge, OS and duration constraints, and discusses challenges like feature extraction, requirement elicitation, and repairs for unsatisfiable requirements.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Sebastian Lubos; Markus Tautschnig; Alexander Felfernig; Viet-Man Le

column3: Configuration manager: describing an emerging professional figure
column10: The paper studies the Configuration Manager professional role, competencies, and training for implementing and managing product configurators and mass-customization systems, focusing on organizational design and pre-deployment implementation/maintenance rather than post-deployment operator troubleshooting. The primary actors are managers/trainers and the artifacts are configurator implementations and business processes, not runtime incident diagnosis in deployed systems.
column11: Defines and characterizes the emerging professional figure of the Configuration Manager by describing their activities, required knowledge and skills, standard descriptors, and proposed training content for implementing and managing product configuration systems and mass customization strategies.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Enrico Sandrin; Cipriano Forza; Gerhard Leitner; Alessio Trentin

column3: Interactive feature modeling with background knowledge for validation and configuration
column10: The paper focuses on design-time interactive feature modeling and validation for product engineers in a manufacturing/software product line context, explicitly describing model editing, background knowledge in FO(ôñ), and interactive configuration rather than post-deployment/runtime troubleshooting by operators. It targets creating and validating designs (feature models and configurations) before deployment, not diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in running systems.
column11: It presents FM-IDP, a tool that extends feature models with expressive background knowledge (FO(ôñ)) and integrates the IDP-Z3 reasoning engine and an interactive explainable interface to help engineers validate models and generate correct configurations for product design.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Simon Vandevelde; Benjamin Callewaert; Joost Vennekens

column3: A generic knowledge model for resource reconfiguration in the context of reconfigurable manufacturing systems
column10: The paper explicitly targets reconfiguration during the exploitation (post-deployment) lifetime of Reconfigurable Manufacturing Systems and describes operators/decision-makers and MES/ERP as actors using the model to reconfigure running manufacturing resources and their settings. It treats real-time, operational configuration (resource settings, flows, reconfiguration time) rather than design-time variability or compile-time checks.
column11: It proposes a knowledge-based system (UML knowledge model + CSP) to support decision-makers in generating and evaluating resource reconfiguration solutions for running reconfigurable manufacturing systems, using real-time inputs (demand, resource health, current settings) and computing metrics such as reconfiguration time to pick suitable operational configurations.
column12: This work computes new valid configurations when system requirements change, whereas our study investigates how practitioners diagnose configuration failures after deployment.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Mathis Allibe; Abdourahim Sylla; Gülgün Alpan

column3: Black-box optimization in a configuration system
column10: The paper studies pre-deployment product configuration in the Merlin CPQ system: manufacturers/suppliers and end-users define objective functions and search for optimal variant selections during configuration/quotation time, not operators troubleshooting live/production systems. It does not address diagnosing, localizing, or repairing misconfigurations in running systems or operator workflows.
column11: It implements and evaluates a black-box, derivative-free optimization module (brute force and coordinate search) inside the Merlin configurator to automatically find near-optimal product configurations across categorical and numeric attributes according to user-defined objective functions, and measures runtime and overhead on different workspaces.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Maximilian Kucher; Tomás Balyo; Noemi Christensen

column3: On the Reuse of Existing Configurations for Testing Evolving Feature Models
column10: The paper studies evolving feature models and reuse of combinatorial test suites for Software Product Lines, focusing on model-based test generation and artefacts (feature models and test suites) handled by testers/developers; it does not address runtime/production misconfiguration diagnosis or operator troubleshooting of deployed systems.
column11: It proposes the Generation From Existing tests (GFE) technique implemented in pMEDICI+ to incrementally reuse and repair existing combinatorial test suites when feature models evolve, and introduces a dissimilarity metric and experimental evaluation comparing GFE to generation-from-scratch.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Andrea Bombarda; Silvia Bonfanti; Angelo Gargantini

column3: Should I Bother? Fast Patch Filtering for Statically-Configured Software Variants
column10: The paper targets post-deployment patch management for 'shipped variants' and explicitly integrates into the patch-management lifecycle after deployment (it collects a Line-Range DB during variant deployment and runs checks when patches arrive), aimed at maintainers/operators handling deployed artifacts. It operates on real deployed/compiled artifacts (CPP-managed C/C++ source for shipped variants) rather than on design-time configuration models.
column11: It proposes SiB, a heuristic that records the source-code line ranges included in deployed statically-configured variants and matches incoming unified-diff patches against that Line-Range DB to efficiently and safely filter which shipped variants are affected, reducing unnecessary builds, tests, and deployments.
column12: This work determines whether a patch affects a configuration variant to guide deployment decisions, whereas our work investigates how practitioners diagnose configuration failures after deployment.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Tobias Landsberg; Christian Dietrich; Daniel Lohmann

column3: On the Use of Multi-valued Decision Diagrams to Count Valid Configurations of Feature Models
column10: The paper addresses design-time analysis of Software Product Lines by compiling feature models into Multi-Valued Decision Diagrams to count valid products; it operates on feature models and benchmarks (synthetic and industrial) rather than on deployed/running systems. The actors are model engineers/researchers and the activity is knowledge compilation/model analysis, not post-deployment operator troubleshooting or runtime misconfiguration diagnosis.
column11: The paper proposes an MDD-based mapping and several optimizations (group merging, dynamic variable re-ordering, constraint merging) to efficiently build decision diagrams and compute the number of valid configurations of feature models, and evaluates scalability and performance compared to BDD-based approaches on synthetic and industrial feature models.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Andrea Bombarda; Angelo Gargantini

column3: Towards a Product Configuration Representation for the Universal Variability Language
column10: The paper addresses product configuration/derivation in Software Product Line engineering using variability models (UVL), which is a design-time modeling and tool support activity rather than post-deployment operational troubleshooting. The actors are engineers configuring UVL models and the artifacts are variability model representations and configuration sequences, not runtime configuration files or operator incident diagnosis in deployed systems.
column11: It proposes a unified, standalone configuration representation for the Universal Variability Language to express configuration sequences, relevancy/visibility conditions, and role-based access control for product derivation tools. The initial proposal focuses on representing configuration sequences and outlines plans for RBAC integration, constraint analysis, a prototype tool, and user evaluation.
column16: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
column21: Hafiyyan Sayyid Fadhlillah; Rick Rabiser
